title: .gitignore: add go.work.sum
body:
Do not commit this file, renovate cannot keep it up to date currently and basically any go test/build command seems to modify this file causing extra work for me to revert the changes and exclude them from my commits as I tend to use git commit -a.

There doesn't seem any strong reason for having this committed in the first place so avoid some churn.

<!--- Please read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) before proceeding --->

comments.body:
> There doesn't seem any strong reason for having this committed in the first place so avoid some churn.

It seems to have the same sort of function as `go.sum`, protecting against unexpected tag moves. That _is_ useful, and worth preserving _if possible_.

@jankaluza is there a good chance of Renovate being fixed / updated in the short term (a week, a few weeks at most)?

I’d prefer keeping the file in git, but if we can’t get Renovate to do maintain it, I agree that the hassle of manually excluding it all of the time is unreasonable (and Git hooks seemingly can auto-add a file on commit in principle, but not “easily”, only in a convoluted way by triggering an immediate `commit --amend`, that also seems not to be worth it).

> It seems to have the same sort of function as go.sum, protecting against unexpected tag moves. That is useful, and worth preserving if possible.

But the file is still generated locally, it is just not committed.
The official go docs do not recommend committing go.work even, https://go.dev/ref/mod#workspaces

And there is https://go.dev/ref/mod#checksum-database so the checksum are still verified when it is first downloaded regardless of if you have it in go.sum or not
And we still have all the go.sum files committed so personally I am a bit confused what go.work.sum protects us against exactly.

> > It seems to have the same sort of function as go.sum, protecting against unexpected tag moves. That is useful, and worth preserving if possible.
> 
> But the file is still generated locally, it is just not committed.

Fair point, and the likelihood of any person <s>ignoring a</s> deleting the file to avoid a match failure is ~the same for committed and uncommitted files. (I think there’s little hope of reviewers noticing that.)

> The official go docs do not recommend committing go.work even, https://go.dev/ref/mod#workspaces

… Yes, but once we are committing `go.work`, I think it makes sense to protect the `go.work` configuration.

> And there is https://go.dev/ref/mod#checksum-database so the checksum are still verified when it is first downloaded regardless of if you have it in go.sum or not

That’s two mechanisms that should help — you’ve convinced me. Let’s get this in, and plan to revert after Renovate is fixed.

> what go.work.sum protects us against exactly.

https://go.googlesource.com/proposal/+/master/design/45713-workspace.md#files the yellow box.

>  and plan to revert after Renovate is fixed.

yes agree, I file an issue to not forget.
